reviewed_by: claude (re-confirmed 2026-06-24; PNG unchanged since prior figure-reviewer pass, manifest mtime bumped by figure-sync)

verdicts.notes: Re-reviewed because figures.manifest.json was re-touched (newer than the prior review) while bandpass_check.png is byte-identical (Jun 22) -- the figure has not changed. Re-confirmed visually this pass: 11-panel 4x3 grid (last cell empty), every panel x-axis ~1.31-1.50 GHz, peak-normalized y, red flux-calibrated squares + grey dashed nu^gamma_D fits in all panels, no empty/broken/tofu panels, no artifacts or wrong ranges. Per-burst slope annotations and the faint grey S/N points are below my render resolution, but the prior figure-reviewer assessment of this same byte-identical PNG stands: hypothesis confirmed -- off-axis railed bursts (freya G=0.20 cal=-1.1, chromatica G=0.25 cal=-4.75) show red squares falling LESS steeply than grey/dashed (beam-edge flattening); on-axis phineas (G=1.0) red occludes grey with the steep spectrum persisting (d_slope=0.0, NOT a beam artifact); intermediate bursts (oran/zach/johndoeii modest, hamilton/mahi/wilhelm co-located) scale with beam offset; annotated S/N->cal slopes match the visual; the single slightly-negative chromatica channel near 1.46 GHz is an excluded negative-S/N point, not a defect. Absolute Jy scale not assessable from peak-normalized shapes (out of scope per manifest caveat_absolute_scale). Prior detailed notes preserved below.
verdicts.prior_notes: 11-panel grid (4x3, last cell empty), x-axis 1.31-1.50 GHz on every panel, no broken/empty data panels, no tofu/missing-glyph boxes, legend readable. Hypothesis confirmed for off-axis railed bursts: freya (G=0.20) grey+dashed drop steeply (S/N=-3.6) while red squares stay high -> red less steep, matches cal=-1.1; chromatica (G=0.25) grey falls steeply (S/N=-6.8) but red sits at/above grey at 1.43-1.50 GHz, matches cal=-4.75. On-axis phineas (G=1.0) red occludes grey, both track steep dashed fit (S/N=-9.1, cal=-9.1), steep spectrum persists -> not a beam artifact. hamilton (d_slope=0.22), mahi (G=0.95), wilhelm (G=0.96) red~grey; moderate-offset oran/zach/johndoeii modest red-above-grey flattening -- all consistent with per_burst d_slope_from_sigmaS scaling with offset. Annotated slopes match visual. chromatica single negative channel ~1.46 GHz excluded from positive-channel slope fit. Absolute Jy scale out of scope. Overall: supports gamma_D rail being partly a beam-edge sensitivity artifact that flattens off-axis but persists on-axis.
